So the query/mark operations are kept in a sequence and handled in reverse order, which would convert splitting operations into union operations. 2.5 AOJ 0189 Problem Code All-pairs shortest path: the Floyd-Warshall algorithm. POJ 3259 Problem Code Single-source shortest path: the Bellman-Ford algorithm. Negative cycle detection using the Bellman-Ford algorithm. A global source vertex numbered 0 0 0 is added to the vertex set. N N N edges, from vertex 0 0 0 to vertex 1 , 2 , … , N 1, 2, \dots, N 1 , 2 , … , N are added to the edge set. Vertex 0 0 0 is used as the single-source in the Bellman-Ford algorithm. Time complexity analysis: In this problem, the graph contains at most V = N = 500 V = N = 500 V = N = 500 vertices and E = M ∗ 2 + W = 5200 E = M * 2 + W = 5200 E = M ∗ 2 + W = 5200 edges. If the Floyd-Warshall algorithm is used to detect negative cycle, the time complexity would be O ( V 3 ) \mathcal{O}(V^3) O ( V 3 ) . Since V 3 ≈ 1 0 8 V^3 \approx 10^8 V 3 ≈ 1 0 8 , it is unacceptable. The time complexity of the Bellman-Ford algorithm is O ( V ⋅ E ) \mathcal{O}(V \cdot E) O ( V ⋅ E ) . Since V ⋅ E ≈ 1 0 6 V \cdot E \approx 10^6 V ⋅ E ≈ 1 0 6 , it is acceptable. CAUTION: Pay attention to the statement: "Two fields might be connected by more than one path". That's to say, if there are more than one path between two fields, the path with minimum time cost should be taken as the edge. Since the final plan must corresponds to a tree rooted by the capital, we just need to keep the cost of the incoming edge to a city. And if two incoming edges both result in the minimum distance, we should choose the one with less cost. AOJ 2200 Problem Code The Floyd-Warshall algorithm and dynamic programming. docs.text: Problem Code Single-source shortest path: the Bellman-Ford algorithm. Negative cycle detection using the Bellman-Ford algorithm. A global source vertex numbered 0 0 0 is added to the vertex set. N N N edges, from vertex 0 0 0 to vertex 1 , 2 , … , N 1, 2, \dots, N 1 , 2 , … , N are added to the edge set. Vertex 0 0 0 is used as the single-source in the Bellman-Ford algorithm. Time complexity analysis: In this problem, the graph contains at most V = N = 500 V = N = 500 V = N = 500 vertices and E = M ∗ 2 + W = 5200 E = M * 2 + W = 5200 E = M ∗ 2 + W = 5200 edges. If the Floyd-Warshall algorithm is used to detect negative cycle, the time complexity would be O ( V 3 ) \mathcal{O}(V^3) O ( V 3 ) . Since V 3 ≈ 1 0 8 V^3 \approx 10^8 V 3 ≈ 1 0 8 , it is unacceptable. The time complexity of the Bellman-Ford algorithm is O ( V ⋅ E ) \mathcal{O}(V \cdot E) O ( V ⋅ E ) . Since V ⋅ E ≈ 1 0 6 V \cdot E \approx 10^6 V ⋅ E ≈ 1 0 6 , it is acceptable. CAUTION: Pay attention to the statement: "Two fields might be connected by more than one path". That's to say, if there are more than one path between two fields, the path with minimum time cost should be taken as the edge.

docs.text: Problem Code Sieve of Eratosthenes, Precomputation, Prefix Sum. In this problem, an H-semi-prime number is an H-number which is the product of exact two H-primes.
